description: Relevance benchmark queries against the code_index codebase. 'target' is the canonical_name the retrieval system should return as the best hit. 'category' tags the expected retrieval personality: literal (BM25 should dominate), semantic (embeddings should help), or mixed.

queries.rationale: Uses the exact symbol name and a content-word. BM25 should trivially hit.
Bare identifier match. BM25 weight on symbol_name is 10x content.

queries.text: function that finds which files to index while respecting ignore rules
queries.rationale: Natural-language description with no direct identifier overlap.

queries.text: compute cosine similarity between a query vector and stored chunk vectors
queries.rationale: Describes mechanism; function name is just 'search'.

queries.text: how do we figure out what breaks when a function changes
queries.rationale: Blast-radius paraphrase with no term overlap with 'impact'.

queries.text: backfill cross-file calls that could not be resolved on first parse
queries.rationale: Docstring paraphrase; identifier starts with underscore.

queries.text: walk a BFS graph from test functions down to the symbol they exercise
queries.rationale: Algorithmic description; no obvious identifier overlap.
'MCP' and 'token' are literal; 'auth' is semantic for 'bearer'.

queries.text: class that decides whether a path matches gitignore patterns
queries.rationale: Concept description with one literal keyword ('gitignore').

queries.text: classify a natural-language question into an intent
queries.rationale: Function name is 'classify'; description uses common words.

queries.text: run the tests command and return affected pytest node ids
queries.rationale: 'tests' and 'pytest node ids' are literal; 'affected' is concept.
